MPCurver Package Overview for Future Agents

What This Package Is

MPCurver implements the MPCurve model for trajectory / pseudotime inference in high-dimensional data. The package is now CAVI-first:

  • The main public workflow is fit_mpcurve() and do_mpcurve().
  • The main algorithm under active development is cavi().
  • Dual-ordering / feature-partition work should happen in the CAVI partition path, not in the older EM code.

As of 2026-04-03, the package is release-clean locally:

  • Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' passes
  • R CMD build . passes
  • R CMD check --no-manual MPCurver_0.2.0.tar.gz returns Status: OK

Public Surface

For new work, treat these as the supported user-facing entry points:

  • fit_mpcurve() in R/07_mpcurve.R
  • do_mpcurve() in R/07_mpcurve.R
  • plot.mpcurve(), summary.mpcurve(), as_mpcurve()

Key public modeling modes:

  • intrinsic_dim = 1: single-ordering CAVI fit
  • intrinsic_dim >= 2: partition-CAVI / multi-ordering fit

fit_mpcurve() is intentionally CAVI-only now. Legacy smooth_em and csmooth_em functions are still present for compatibility, but they are not the main package workflow and should not be the default direction for new changes.

Main Files

If you are changing the recommended path, start here:

  • R/07_mpcurve.R: public wrapper and unified mpcurve object
  • R/09_cavi.R: single-ordering CAVI implementation
  • R/10_partition_cavi.R: dual-ordering / partition CAVI implementation
  • R/01_initialization.R: ordering methods and initialization helpers
  • R/02_prior.R: random-walk precision constructors
  • R/00_utils.R: shared math / cache helpers

Supporting docs:

  • README.Rmd: top-level public positioning and examples
  • vignettes/mpcurve_intro.Rmd: current single-ordering introduction
  • vignettes/partition.Rmd: current partition / dual-ordering vignette
  • important_derivations/: repo-level math notes for the CAVI path

Object Model

Important classes:

  • cavi: main active algorithm object
  • mpcurve: unified user-facing wrapper around a raw fit
  • csmooth_em, smooth_em: legacy compatibility classes

For a cavi object, the fields that matter most are:

  • $params: current pi and sigma2
  • $posterior$mean: posterior mean trajectories
  • $posterior$cov: per-feature posterior covariance matrices
  • $posterior$diag: posterior marginal variances
  • $gamma: responsibilities over latent positions
  • $lambda_vec: feature-specific smoothness values
  • $elbo_trace: primary monotone objective
  • $loglik_trace: plug-in diagnostic only, not the monotonicity certificate

Active Development Guidance

Please keep these package decisions intact unless there is a strong reason to change them:

  • Public workflow stays centered on fit_mpcurve() / do_mpcurve()
  • cavi is the active development target
  • mpcurve remains the unified public return type
  • Legacy csmooth_em / smooth_em code stays compatibility-only
  • Do not re-promote legacy algorithms in the README or top-level vignettes

For correctness work in cavi, keep these invariants in mind:

  • The E-step analogue must include the posterior uncertainty correction
  • $elbo_trace should be the quantity used for monotonicity checks
  • $loglik_trace is only a diagnostic
  • Feature-specific lambda_j updates depend on the posterior second moment, not a pure MAP plug-in rule

Legacy Boundary

Legacy files are:

  • R/03_SmoothEM.R
  • R/04_methods.R
  • R/06_cSmoothEM.R
  • R/07_partition.R
  • R/08_SoftSmoothEM.R

These remain in the repo because they are useful for backward compatibility, benchmarking, and regression tests. They are not the recommended place for new feature work.

If you need to touch them:

  • Prefer compatibility fixes over architectural expansion
  • Avoid making the public docs more legacy-centric again
  • Keep class names stable for downstream users

Performance Hotspots

The main performance hotspot is still in R/09_cavi.R:

  • per-feature posterior updates
  • repeated Cholesky / inverse work
  • storage of full S_j even when only diagonal or trace terms are needed

If optimizing, preserve correctness first, especially uncertainty terms.

Validation Commands

Use these before concluding a substantial change:

Rscript -e 'roxygen2::roxygenise()'
Rscript -e 'rmarkdown::render("README.Rmd")'
Rscript -e 'devtools::test()'
R CMD build .
R CMD check --no-manual MPCurver_0.2.0.tar.gz

If the version changes, update the tarball name in the last command.
